title: Logigraph rule node
description: One node = one business rule. References domain entities; claims depgraph code nodes that implement the rule. The plain-language statement and Decision table in the dossier are the canonical statement of meaning; the technical anchor is one implementation of it.

title.description: Short human-readable label. E.g. "Peer crew identity hidden unless resume is published".

statement.description: Single-sentence plain-English rule. The full structured form (Decision table, examples, edge cases) lives in the dossier.

references_domain.description: Domain node ids the rule mentions. Validated against nodes/domain/ at regen time; missing ids are orphans.

claims_code.description: Depgraph code nodes that implement this rule. At least one required — a rule with zero claims is unenforced.

fan_out.description: Computed at regen time: count of claims_code entries. Reported by bin/logigraph fan-out for refactor signals.

confidence.description: How confident the author is in the rule's accuracy. Low = forward-looking or speculative.

source.description: Where the rule was sourced from (memory file, design doc, code docstring).

structural_hash.description: SHA-256 over statement + references_domain + claim ids. Drift triggers dossier re-review.

dossier.description: Path to the plain-language dossier markdown (relative to logigraph/).

flagged.description: Set by `bin/logigraph flag`. true = this rule documents a known deficiency or contested decision; injection surfaces a FLAGGED banner before the rule body.

flagged_by.description: Who flagged. Written by `flag` from --actor or $USER.

tracked.description: true = formally acknowledged, fix planned or accepted as-is; false = fresh signal needing triage.

tracking_ref.description: Optional pointer — plan path, git sha, ticket id, or free-text reference for tracked warnings.

affected.description: Optional list of node ids this warning cross-references.

depgraph_id.description: Id of a node in the depgraph corpus. Validated at regen — missing ids are orphans.

role.description: How this code relates to the rule. enforces=gate/filter, checks=predicate, serializes=response shape, displays=UI surface, emits=side-effect, illustrates=cited as example/witness when primary enforcement surfaces aren't depgraph-tracked.

where.description: File:line range where the rule lives in this node's source. E.g. "events.py:2235-2406".

remote_hash.description: Copy of the depgraph node's structural_hash at last review. Drift between this and current hash flags the claim as stale.

stale.description: Set by reconcile when remote_hash diverges from depgraph node. Surfaced by hooks; cleared by re-reviewing the claim.
